title: How to create a PDF document
description: Learn how to save files (like word documents or web pages) as a PDF so they can be opened by anyone.

steps.title: Open your document
steps.body: Open the document, picture, or webpage you want to convert in your word processor (like Word or Google Docs) or browser.

steps.title: Open the Print menu
steps.body: Hold down the 'Control' key and press 'P' (or 'Command' and 'P' on a Mac). Alternatively, click File -> Print.

steps.title: Change Destination to Save as PDF
steps.body: Look for the setting that lists your paper printer. Click it and change the selection to 'Save as PDF' or 'Microsoft Print to PDF'.

steps.title: Click Save
steps.body: Tap the blue 'Save' (or 'Print') button. A box will open asking where to save the file on your computer. Choose a folder and click Save.

glossary.definition: A file type (stands for Portable Document Format) that locks the formatting of your pages so it looks exactly the same on any screen or computer.
A feature on computers that creates a digital PDF file instead of printing pages out on real paper.
